diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers kept the claim unsupported and used no citations, so there is no citation difference to diagnose. The main difference is the reason-code framing: option_1 gives a broader lack-of-support diagnosis ('insufficient_evidence'), while option_2 gives a more claim-targeted formulation ('central_proposition_unsupported') that focuses on the specific proposition that full exposition is an exception. These are not in conflict; option_2 is narrower and more specific, while option_1 is a general umbrella rationale. The written rationales are likewise aligned: both say the available evidence does not establish the claimed exception, with option_2 adding slightly more interpretation about past preference/verbosity. Confidence is identical at high.
